summary: Agatha gently shook her head, giving up. “She’ll be here in an hour. But you have to promise me to behave yourself.” “What do you think I’ll do?” “Hit her?” Agatha almost grinned, “Mark your territory and intimidate her?” “It’s my wife and kid we’re talking about–” “Ex.” “What?” Agatha narrowed her eyes, a certain pain lingering behind the aqua. “Ex wife, Rio. I’m your ex wife, you're my ex wife.” “I said that.” “No,” She blew out a tired breath. “You didn’t.” OR Agathario have been divorced for 3yrs and Rio has never fully accepted it, until Agatha gets serious about someone else. Rio has to move and acknowledge that they're out of time and the best days are behind them...or does she?

Just another divorced-wives AU. It’s been ten years since Agatha signed the papers on the life she and Rio were building, convincing herself it was the only way to let Rio chase the career she deserved. Now they’re in the same room for the first time since the divorce, at a fiftieth birthday party, and Agatha is fighting a war between what she wants, what she’s terrified to admit, and what she still believes everybody else needs.

Rio is the one to roll her eyes when the woman looks down at her watch again. It’s no fun if she isn’t engaging. “So… who’s the lucky date?” “What does it matter to you?” she snaps, and Rio does roll her eyes again, right in front of her. The goal is to set her off, isn’t it? And the woman is getting on her nerves. “Well, I know what daisies mean. It’s thoughtful,” Rio explains. “They’re… pure. They signify new beginnings. You’re dressed well, and you’re impatient. The date must mean enough to you. And you want this bouquet to be nice, don’t you? So tell me more.” Or, Rio owns the local flowershop, and she can't help but be curious about her newest customer. Agatha shows every week, needing flowers to bring to her dates, and... Rio is interested, even when it should be a massive red flag.

Tonight, Rio had agreed-reluctantly-to go out.Rio hadn’t been able to say no when Alice had asked her to come out “for just one drink.” That one drink had snowballed into a full-blown descent into chaos. She was drunk. Blackout levels of drunk. And in that delicious haze, with her limbs loose and her thoughts looser, she saw her.

”I barely touched it.” Rio sternly grabs her wrist again pulling it into the stream insistently while Agatha struggles against her grip. ”You need to put it under cold water-“ ”-It doesn’t even hurt-“ “-Stop being awkward-“ ”-I’m fine, really-“ ”-Agatha, don’t move.” And that’s when she hears it. Don’t move. You don’t want it badly enough yet. The air leaves Agatha’s lungs in a rush. She hears every filthy word she’s heard whispered in her ear as she climbs higher and higher echoing in her brain. The faceless woman who’s been talking her to orgasm. Except now she has a face. It’s Rio. When Agatha Harkness discovers audio erotica she finally gets something that forty-two years of life experience and a god-awful marriage never got her. But suddenly she finds herself with a roommate with the most familiar voice.

“You’re early,” Agatha managed, feigning a flicker of annoyance, though her pulse quickened. "I missed you.” The words were simple, almost soft. Her dark gaze held Agatha’s, steady and unyielding. "Agatha huffed,  “Is that so?” She turned away, trying to mask the slight flush rising to her cheeks. "I’d think the Queen of Shadows wouldn’t be so sentimental.” The woman’s lips curved ever so slightly as she closed the distance between them. “Think what you want. But here you are." / or : Hadestown came on shuffle, thought about the Hades/Persephone Rio/Agatha parallels and things spiraled wildly out of control

“Why does this ridiculous little family have to include me? Sounds like you’re just making up reasons to be upset,” Agatha said, pursing her lips to try and keep in character, to not let her performance slip. “Because it’s you, Agatha," Billy said, not letting go of Agtha's hands. "Because I love you and you matter to me. You matter, Agatha. You always have, that has never changed. You are the heart of this family, no matter what you pretend to be otherwise to keep us at arm’s length. Our beating, bleeding, broken heart that we need to stay alive.” “I’m not broken,” Agatha grumbled, not hating the analogy as much as she should. “Quit writing me poetry.” “No, Agatha, you’re not broken. But we hurt you, we broke your heart, and I won’t pretend that we didn’t. Please give us a chance, give me a chance to make this right. Just one more time. And if you hate it, I promise to never ask you again. Just one more tour.” -- Or, Fleetwood Mac rewritten as ex-wives Agatha Harkness and Rio Vidal. It's been 10 years since the band broke up-- can anything be salvaged?

Another Celebrity AU! Rio had never been lucky. If she ordered food, they’d forget the sauce. She always picked the slowest checkout line, and if there was one chair with gum on it, her butt would find it. If a celebrity was going to accidentally add the wrong person on Snapchat, it should have been someone cooler, someone who didn’t still trip over their own shoelaces. But one fateful Tuesday evening, luck finally threw her a bone in the form of an accidental Snapchat add. Here she was, staring at her phone screen in absolute disbelief. Agatha Harkness has added you as a friend.

This is the sequel to Scherzo, But it can stand alone! Hot, older composition professor Rio is now married to chaotic conducting student Agatha, and they’re expecting a baby. These are scenes from the pregnancy. A one-shot for each trimester.

Agatha and her friends have been planning their holiday getaway for months, and the trip is almost upon them. Better yet, Agatha promised them that they would finally get to meet her girlfriend on this trip. There’s only one problem: Agatha has been lying to them. She hasn’t dated anyone in over a year. Enter Rio Vidal. Agatha’s coworker is skeptical of this plan, but after a little convincing, she agrees to being the fake girlfriend. Now all that’s left to do is survive the vacation. In a grab for political power, Rio finds herself in an arranged marriage with the heir to the Harkness Empire. Resigned to her duties as a filial daughter, Rio believes she will be able to swallow her pride and live in a loveless marriage for the rest of her life. That is until she meets her soon-to-be sister-in-law, the black sheep of the family, Agatha.

Almost a year after first turning up on Valentine's Day with a baby raccoon in tow, the lives of Rio and Agatha Harkness-Vidal have been anything but normal. A year of laughter, a year of adventure, and a year of unforeseen circumstance after unforeseen circumstance have all culminated in a home life neither of them could have predicted. While sticking to their mandate of never having children, it's become an after-thought to the ones they've found. In indulging both the animals and her own inner child, Agatha provides a little fun and chaos for the five of them and a new friend appears, leaving Rio wonder if life, in any way, could ever be any better.

Pop icon and lead singer of Sisters of the Craft, Agatha Harkness is the epitome of too cool for you, especially when it comes to Lady Death, rockstar and one half of the rock duo The Court, who just loves to antagonize Agatha unprovoked. Lady Death who is also her wife. A story in three parts, followed by a series of epilogues because why not
Agatha and Rio are engaged and ready to start their life together, but just because they're adults doesn't mean they can't rush into a few things.

When Agatha makes a bad decision and has to suffer the consequences, she isn't the only one suffering. Rio loves her wife more than anything in the world, but Agatha's lack of ability in the bedroom leads to her mounting frustration and inability to curb her tongue. When Zap makes a new friend that Agatha highly disapproves of, things are said that aren't meant and the road to reconciliation appears to be shorter than it might have at first.

Agatha is a legacy at Westview Academy, born into privilege in terms of wealth but not in terms of affection or love of any kind. She does what she can to fit in according to her parents' standards, but there's a basketball player at her school that might be turning that all on its head. Agatha All Along Week Day Seven: Forbidden AU Oh, what was that? You thought I WASN'T going to incorporate my love for Selena into a fanfiction that is literally ABOUT forbidden love? ha. HA.

The Darkhold's magic wavers for a mere moment as Wanda completes her enchantment over the town of Westview, yet a mere moment is all it takes for the shroud of Agatha's secrecy to slip and for Rio to find her. Agatha's camouflage fails long before Wanda steals her magic, Rio find her at the beginning of Wandavision, chaos ensues. X Rio however, didn’t look upset at all to see her. If anything she looked thrilled, shooting a wolfish grin at Agatha over Wanda’s shoulder, as if to say found you bitch . “Hello,” Wanda’s voice, which startled Agatha back into the present, was both infuriatingly pleasant and welcoming, “can I help you with something?” Rio’s sharp grin remained in place as those dark eyes of hers continued their slow perusal of Agatha's figure, taking her in from the bottom of her kitten heels, to the perfectly pinned dark curls around her face. At long last, her gaze slid away from holding Agatha hostage to answer Wanda’s question. “Hi neighbour, I was just passing by and thought it polite to introduce myself. I’m Rita Valdez, I live at number 17, just right down the street from here”.

“Oh my god,” Billy says, and when Rio glances up, they’re all staring at her: Billy, Eddie, Alice, Jen. “You and Agatha went on a date.” Rio scoffs. “It wasn’t a date. It was just dinner.” “So you just rolled out of bed and let her buy you an expensive dinner?” Jen says, which makes Alice snort. “Sounds like you have a sugar mama,” Alice says. “I guess that’s one way of surviving academia.” They all laugh, and Alice raises her water glass to salute Rio. “No judgment,” she adds. “I would kill for a hot older professor buying me dinner and helping me with my writing.” - Rio's POV.

Rio, as a nun, suffers from nightmares and strange dreams, thinking that she is being haunted by a sleep paralysis demon. At the same time, the mysterious woman from the church falls in love with Rio and tries to do everything to persuade her to sin.

Rio never fails to surprise her wife. It had started with an impulsive purchase of an ejaculating strap-on and has since escalated in the most thrilling ways. Something's on Rio's mind and Agatha's determined to find out what it is. Or, sex, love, unholy truth part 2!

“i'm so glad you’ve come,” she said to rio as she led her out onto the porch. “meeting you has made my day.” “it has?” rio leaned a little closer to her, conspiratorially. “well, how pleasant and uncommon it is to make someone’s day.” an agathario pioneer au based on the world to come .

“You look like you just sprinted a mile,” Alice said, smirking. “Dr. Harkness give you a hard time today?” Rio dropped into the seat across from her, pulling her scarf loose and letting out a sigh. “No, not really. She actually said I had a ‘fair interpretation.’” Alice leaned forward, resting her chin on her hand. “And that made your entire week, didn’t it?” Rio tried to brush it off, stirring sugar into her coffee. “She’s just — she has this presence, you know? The way she talks, the way she challenges you. It’s like she’s… magnetic.” “Uh-huh.” Alice’s smirk deepened. “Magnetic. Sure. That’s the word we’re going with?” “Oh, stop it,” Rio groaned, her cheeks heating. ------- Or: Rio Vidal, a PhD student, finds herself enthralled with Dr. Agatha Harkness, her Modernist poetry seminar teacher, and her adorable son, Nicky.

"Do you want to make it up to me, Riley?" Another nod, more enthusiastic this time. Eve's smile only grew, and Riley could've cried with relief as she reached up and pulled her into a kiss. Rio stood, her movements slow and deliberate, like a predator that didn’t need to rush. "It means," she said, stepping toward her, "there are things about yourself, about us, that you’ve forgotten." Something cold curled around Agatha’s spine. "But you’re starting to remember now," Rio continued, her voice softer, more knowing. "Aren’t you?" And suddenly, she was. The image hit her like a slap, flashes of a dense forest, a hand gripping hers, laughter echoing in the trees, a warmth that felt like home. It was there, just out of reach, teasing at the edges of her mind. She staggered back, her breath catching. "What... what are you?" she whispered, voice barely audible. Rio’s lips curled into a faint smile. "That’s the right question, darling." Her eyes gleamed. "And when you’re ready to hear the answer, I’ll tell you." Leaning slightly forward, she came closer to her head and hair, strongly smelling of lavender and sandalwood. She cleared her throat and scratched her nose instead of inhaling it. “Go slow, no need to rush if you haven’t done this in a while,” Rio mumbled as she arranged the sheets. “Oh,” Agatha grinned, “I always go slow when I can Miss Vidal,” she replied and placed her hands on the keyboard, hitting the first notes with a surety Rio hadn’t expected. All witches possess wings, which are a reflection of their soul and magic. On the night of Samhain, 1675, Agatha Harkness is born with black wings; the wings of a crow, but mistaken to be the wings of a raven. Wings of Death. She is ostracized by the coven flock and raised to believe Death has marked her evil, sinful soul, and will one day come to claim her. What she does not know is that Evanora is partially correct. Death will, one day, claim her - because after millennia of waiting, Death's Soulmate has finally been born.

Rio Vidal, a legal assistant at a prestigious firm in New York, lives between hard work and parties filled with music and plenty of alcohol. One fun night, she and her friends create a fake profile on a social media platform to tease their strict boss, Agatha Harkness. What starts as an innocent prank escalates into unimaginable proportions, and Rio finds herself in a dilemma between keeping her secret identity and facing the emotional realities of her job.

When Rio joined Agatha on the witches' road, she wasn’t scared. If anything, she was cocky. The idea of walking the witches' road with her wife again felt like an adventure—an opportunity to be by Agatha’s side, even if Agatha clearly didn’t want her there. Rio thought it would be fun, familiar, despite the trials ahead. She had convinced herself nothing could touch her. But when the second trial arrived, everything changed. Rio found herself standing on the porch of the only home she had ever known—the home she shared with Agatha and their son, Nicholas. The witches’ road was ancient, as old as she was, and it had reached into her mind, plucked her deepest fear from the darkness she refused to acknowledge. It wasn’t a monster or some forgotten villain. It was a memory.

As teenagers, Rio Vidal and Agatha Harkness fell into the all-consuming throes of young love on Christmas Day, 2002. It was their first kiss and the first of the thousands of kisses they would share over the next ten years of their lives. After years of masters and doctorate programs, their chosen career paths pulled them in opposing directions. Out of fear and misunderstanding, the relationship fell to pieces and left both parties irrevocably injured, never wanting to cross paths again. On Christmas Eve, Rio finds herself stranded with her car stuck in a snow bank and a nine hour waitlist for a tow. With a couple of miles to walk in the frigid cold, Rio begins her trek only to believe that someone has arrived to save her. When she turns around to view her knight, Agatha is the one that climbs out of the car. How much can change in a decade? Maybe, the more important question when it came to Agatha and Rio was instead: How much more could stay the same? Christmas AU One-Shot!

“I think believing in something so bigger than yourself is romantic.” “Romantic?” Agatha asked, heart stuttering. “Yeah,” she said shyly, “to devote yourself so wholeheartedly to something, unconditionally, letting it into every part of your life. I’d say that’s pretty romantic.” Or Agatha was seven years old when she learned that loving women was a sin.

Once upon a time, life was perfect for Agatha and Rio Harkness-Vidal. In their early thirties, they both had thriving careers and a home life that was made immaculate with the arrival of their son, Nicholas. Four months after his birth and after the recruitment of a nanny, Rio arrives home to an empty house and no trace of her son or the nanny. What first seemed hopeful carries on for twenty-five very long years until suddenly, the unthinkable happens. In The Big Apple, a mere car ride away from the Harkness-Vidal home in Newark, a twenty-five year old physical therapist who has always wondered about his mother's avoidance about his other parent decides to take an ancestry test. His girlfriend bought it for him for his birthday and, well, if his mother won't provide answers to his questions, he'll get them on his own. When his sample flags as a match to a two-and-a-half decade old DNA sample from a missing person's case, he gets far more of them than he ever could have imagined.

Rules have never been a difficult concept for Rio Vidal. Following them hasn't either, although she has never been beyond the human experience of making mistakes. By the fifth body, Rio is beginning to saw her nose off to spite her face; not knowing how such a thing keeps happening, even if it is at the hands of another human being, starts to tilt her focus and skew her moral compass. Agatha Harkness is one of the most prominent professors in the Criminal Justice department at Westview University. The first time Agatha saw her, she was stepping over her mother’s corpse. The moment was brief, but Agatha recognized her instantly. She felt a dull sting in the palm of her hand; her mark was burning. It wasn’t a wholly unpleasant sensation, if she was being honest with herself. OR: Every witch in Salem is born with a mark. From the moment she is born, Agatha Harkness knows her mark means that she is bound to the Angel of Death. “I don’t believe you. You prefer me—“ “Compliant?” Rio stood slowly, her eyes never leaving Agatha’s face. The action put her inches away, her body heat radiating out, searing Agatha’s skin. “Obedient?” Agatha’s hips shifted against her will, her lips parting slightly.
